articles.headline: Taiwan, S Korea Climb Global Equity Ranks on AI Boom with TSMC and Samsung Surges
articles.summary: TSMC shares rose over 40% and Samsung/SK Hynix over 80% this year as key Nvidia suppliers, driving Taiwan and South Korea up equity indexes amid broadening AI hardware demand. Google's NT$27 billion investment approval in Taiwan for data processing and semiconductors underscores supply chain commitments. This strengthens global chip availability but heightens reliance on Asian manufacturers for business AI infrastructure.

articles.headline: Tesla Boosts 2026 Capex to Over $25 Billion for AI, Robotaxis, and Humanoid Robots
articles.summary: Tesla plans to triple capital spending to over $25 billion on AI self-driving tech, Optimus robots, and robotaxis, testing investor patience amid negative cash flow. This aggressive pivot signals broader auto industry pressure to invest in AI automation, potentially disrupting labor markets and supply chains for robotics components. Shares dropped 3% as analysts question returns compared to Big Tech's cloud-funded AI strategies.

articles.headline: Maine Gov. Mills Vetoes Data Center Moratorium, Enabling AI Infrastructure Expansion
articles.summary: Maine's governor vetoed a bill for the first U.S. statewide data center pause until 2027, supporting critical growth in digital infrastructure for AI and cloud services. This decision benefits businesses planning expansions in data-heavy regions by avoiding construction delays and costs. It signals regulatory support for AI compute needs amid rising enterprise demand.
